title: pleopeltis polypodioides var. acicularis
intro: Pleopeltis polypodioides var. acicularis, also known as the resurrection fern, is a unique and fascinating plant species that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. It is a type of epiphytic fern that is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America, known for its ability to survive in a desiccated state and revive itself when exposed to water. The resurrection fern has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and terpenoids. Herbal preparations of the resurrection fern can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.
